Export a ggplot in publication-ready format

Source: R/write_manuscript.R
export_figure.Rd

Writes a ggplot2 object to PDF/SVG/PNG/TIFF/EPS at the specified physical dimensions (in millimetres) and resolution.

Usage

export_figure(plot, filename, width_mm = 174, height_mm = 120, dpi = 300)

Arguments

plot

A ggplot2 object.

filename

Character. Output path (extension determines format).

width_mm

Numeric. Width in mm. 174 = Springer single-column, 88 = double-column. Default 174.

height_mm

Numeric. Height in mm. Default 120.

dpi

Integer. Raster resolution. Default 300.

Value

Invisibly, the filename.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
p <- ggplot2::ggplot(mtcars, ggplot2::aes(mpg, hp)) +
  ggplot2::geom_point()
export_figure(p, "mtcars.pdf", width_mm = 120, height_mm = 90)
} # }
